post.description: The optimizer's single-authority setpoint submission (RFC-005). A partial (merge) update of a greenhouse's target bundle: absent fields are left unchanged, a present `zones` array updates the named zones' targets (matched by `zone_id`; unlisted zones unchanged; an unknown `zone_id` rejected 422 — zone topology is fixed), and out-of-range values or cross-field invariants (e.g. humidity_low_pct < humidity_high_pct) are rejected 422 naming the violated bound. A submission is additionally gated to the active crop profile stage's crop-safe envelope — climate targets and the per-zone irrigation targets alike — so a value outside it is rejected 422. Unlike the operator's ad-hoc edit (the `PATCH` counterpart in the `platform-dashboard-rest` contract, catalog #4), a submission here is recorded with `source = optimizer` provenance and returned 202 Accepted — the resulting intended state, delivered to the controller or held when it is offline. Authentication is the config-gated service-auth boundary (RFC-011): with `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=trusted_network` (default) it accepts the untokened internal call; with `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE=oidc` it requires a Keycloak client-credentials token carrying the narrow `setpoints:write` role (or an operator token) and rejects unauthenticated calls 401 / non-privileged 403. The `{}` scheme is listed first, so the token is optional at the contract level — enforcement is gated on `SERVICE_AUTH_MODE`.

202.description: The submission was accepted; the returned bundle is the resulting intended state (delivered to the controller, or held when it is offline).
